pixel art vs regular digital art difference
answers: Pixel art is a form of digital art where images are created at the pixel level. It originated from early video games and remains popular for indie games, retro aesthetics, and NFTs.
Pixel art holo ekta digital art form jekhane ekta ekta pixel place kore art banano hoy. Retro game er moton look — Stardew Valley, Celeste eishob pixel art game.
Tools for pixel art include Aseprite (best for game sprites), Piskel (free, browser-based), GraphicsGale, and even Photoshop. Start small — try 16x16 or 32x32 sprites before going bigger.

Pixel art requires patience and precision. Key techniques include anti-aliasing, dithering for gradients, and limited color palettes. Start with simple objects, then try characters with walk cycle animations.
